baselineDescription: The pre-roadmap regression suite: 26 CPU/unit tests and 7 hardware-browser E2E tests. Phase 0 infrastructure tests are intentionally part of the growing current suite, not this frozen baseline.

expectedTests.selector: assertHardwareWebGPUAdapter > accepts a hardware adapter
assertHardwareWebGPUAdapter > rejects an adapter marked as a fallback
assertHardwareWebGPUAdapter > rejects SwiftShader even when it is not marked as a fallback
TriangleRenderer > encodes one triangle and submits it
TriangleRenderer > does not finish rendering before submitted GPU work is complete
TriangleRenderer > rejects a target with a format that does not match the pipeline
procedural demo scenes > uses the UI scene IDs and defaults to the minimal cantilever
procedural demo scenes > builds every scene within the browser preprocessing budget
procedural demo scenes > is byte-for-byte deterministic
procedural demo scenes > packs dense body metadata and valid mass inputs for COM correction
tetrahedral CPU preprocessing > extracts the deterministic triangulated cuboid boundary
tetrahedral CPU preprocessing > transforms and appends meshes while preserving topology metadata
tetrahedral CPU preprocessing > assembles symmetric linear elasticity and conservative lumped mass
exact JGS2 rest perturbation basis > satisfies S_i Ubar_i = I and complementary equilibrium
exact JGS2 rest perturbation basis > builds ordered orthonormal low-frequency rest-Hessian modes
exact JGS2 rest perturbation basis > matches the selected block of a full Newton solve (Eq. 7-13 oracle)
exact JGS2 rest perturbation basis > matches every global Newton block with local plus all candidate remainders
exact JGS2 rest perturbation basis > keeps every scene's selected-Cubature low-mode solves within 2%
exact JGS2 rest perturbation basis > produces bounded, nonnegative complementary cubature
JGS2 timestep compatibility > accepts values represented by the same GPU f32
JGS2 timestep compatibility > rejects a changed or invalid timestep
JGS2 per-body bookkeeping > allocates body corrections after all per-tetrahedron rotations
JGS2 per-body bookkeeping > infers body count from packed vertexInfo body ids
JGS2 per-body bookkeeping > rejects body ids that would allocate more bodies than vertices
JGS2 contact parameters > accepts a nonnegative damping rate and contact margin
JGS2 contact parameters > rejects invalid damping rates or margins
renders a useful DOM error when WebGPU is unavailable
deterministic JGS2 scenes > minimal: frame 0 to frame 45
deterministic JGS2 scenes > stiffness: frame 0 to frame 60
deterministic JGS2 scenes > drop: frame 0 to frame 90
deterministic JGS2 scenes > stress: frame 0 to frame 60
long-run settled body stability > drop: remains in its lane for 20 simulated seconds
long-run settled body stability > stress: remains in its lane for 20 simulated seconds
